label: Seedance 2 (Video Generation)
description: ByteDance Seedance 2. AI filmmaking video prompts with text-only, storyboard-driven, and character-sheet-plus-storyboard reference modes.

reference_mode.label: Reference Mode
reference_mode.description: Choose text-only prompts, storyboard-grid reference, or character sheets plus storyboard.

subject.label: Subject
subject.description: Who or what is in the shot. Reuse character descriptions verbatim when no reference sheet or Soul ID is available.

action.label: Action / Motion
action.description: What happens. Use strong kinetic verbs and cover the complete 0:00-0:15 timeline when authoring a full sequence.

camera.label: Camera Instruction
camera.description: Single camera direction: slow push in, low tracking shot, static wide, handheld follow. Do not use the word camera.

environment.label: Environment / Lighting
environment.description: Location, light source, and atmosphere in one sentence.

style.label: Style / Technical Notes
style.description: Shared visual style, palette, grain, lens, and rendering notes. Keep consistent across shots.

character_references.label: Character Sheets
character_references.description: Optional character reference sheets. Number them uniquely as @image1, @image2, etc.

storyboard_reference.label: Storyboard Grid
storyboard_reference.description: Optional 9-panel storyboard grid. Use as the main visual and motion reference.

reference_image.label: Start Frame
reference_image.description: Optional single start-frame reference for image-to-video generation.

audio.label: Audio / Mood
audio.description: Audio instruction. Default is NO MUSIC unless the user explicitly requests music or dialog.

negative.label: Negative Prompt
negative.description: What to avoid, such as text on screen, watermark, music, low quality, or identity drift.

defaults.negative: text on screen, captions, watermark, low quality, character drift, inconsistent costume, unwanted music

prompt_assembly.template: FORMAT: {{duration}} seconds / {{aspect}} / {{style}} / {{audio}}. SUBJECT: {{subject}}. ENVIRONMENT: {{environment}}. ACTION AND TIMELINE: {{action}}. CAMERA: {{camera}}. REFERENCES: {{reference_mode}}; character sheets {{character_references}}; storyboard grid {{storyboard_reference}}; start frame {{reference_image}}. NEGATIVE: {{negative}}.

v_setup_injection.note: v_setup tokens are fallback context. Do not duplicate populated camera, style, or environment fields.

llm_instructions.writing_guide: Write Seedance 2 prompts using the AI filmmaking pipeline. Default duration is 15 seconds unless the user explicitly asks for less. Choose the correct reference mode: text for no storyboard, storyboard when a grid is the main reference, characters_and_storyboard when character sheets and a storyboard grid exist. If using multiple references, number every image uniquely: Character A sheet is @image1, Character B sheet is @image2, storyboard grid is the next number. Never reuse @image1 for multiple references. When a storyboard grid exists, prefer one continuous 15-second sequence over separate text-only shots. Default audio is NO MUSIC unless the user requests music, a track, dialog, or sound design. Keep prompts lean, kinetic, and direct.

field_guides.reference_mode: text = Variant A; storyboard = Variant B; characters_and_storyboard = Variant C.
field_guides.subject: Use the exact character-lock description verbatim if no visual reference is available. If a character sheet, Soul ID, or strong reference exists, keep this short and avoid face re-description.
field_guides.action: For text mode, cover the full 0:00-0:15 range with three short timeline beats. For storyboard modes, compress the board into a brief through-line and tell Seedance to read panels sequentially.
field_guides.camera: Single short instruction or brief camera logic. Do not start with the word camera.
field_guides.environment: One sentence: location, time of day, light source, atmosphere.
field_guides.style: Use the same palette, grain, lens, and lighting language across all shots in a sequence.
field_guides.character_references: Assign unique @image numbers to every sheet. Do not collapse multiple characters onto @image1.
field_guides.storyboard_reference: If present, the storyboard grid is the main visual and motion reference. Read panels as sequential shots, not as one image.
field_guides.audio: Default NO MUSIC. Include dialog only when requested. For replies, start with 'She replies:' or 'He replies:' to preserve speaking order.

output.subject: RAVEN: Mid-40s, lean Asian man, silver-grey spiky hair, sleeveless ochre kung-fu robe, gold arm cuffs, barefoot.
output.action: 0:00-0:05: Static wide - Raven waits in fighting stance as dust drifts across the floor. 0:05-0:10: Low tracking shot - Viper lunges, palm strike cutting through the dust. 0:10-0:15: Tight close-up - Raven pivots away, sleeve snapping past the lens.
output.camera: wide to low tracking to tight close-up
output.environment: Abandoned dojo at dusk, broken roof beams, warm side light through dust.
output.style: 35mm film grain, muted ochre palette, grounded martial-arts realism

output.action: Use character sheets @image1 and @image2 and storyboard grid @image3. Create a 15-second cinematic sequence. Read the storyboard panels as sequential shots, not one image. Follow panel order, camera logic, motion arrows, and framing consistently. Handheld moments may add realism. NO TEXT ON SCREEN, NO MUSIC. Storyline: Viper breaks the standoff, Raven evades, both reset in tense stillness.

do: Cover the full 0:00-0:15 timeline in text mode
Use storyboard mode when a grid exists
Keep style language consistent across shots
dont: Default to a shorter duration unless requested
Add music unless the user asks for it
Use the same @image number for different references
Use Variant A when a storyboard grid is available
Put captions or text on screen unless requested
